design_philosophy.core_loop: Launch ball, navigate flippers to keep ball in play, hit targets and bumpers to accumulate score, manage ball loss risk
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Ball physics vs. player reflexes—constant threat of ball loss creates moment-to-moment pressure
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Mastery of arcade pinball mechanics on home console; skillful ball control and high-score chasing
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Faithful adaptation of physical pinball arcade experience to NES hardware; focuses on pure mechanical simulation rather than narrative or progression. No Mario theming despite US marketing, signaling authenticity to source material over IP synergy.

difficulty.lives_system: Multiple balls per game; ball loss mechanic creates finite lives
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High agency through flipper timing and positioning; physics-based skill determines success. No difficulty settings documented, but ball physics provide natural graduated challenge.

replayability_hooks: Bonus game featuring Lady (Pauline) character accessible through table completion
Multiple tables/playfields (suggested by bonus game inclusion)
Arcade-style endless replayability without progression gates

world_building_techniques: Arcade aesthetic and authentic pinball table layout
Character appearance (Lady/Pauline) in bonus game provides minimal narrative flavor
No dialogue or cutscenes; pure mechanical simulation

danger_signals: Tilt mechanic (implied by arcade pinball conventions; risky moves punished)
environmental_storytelling: Arcade cabinet aesthetic communicates 1980s gaming culture
Table layout design guides player flow and risk/reward zones

sources_used: tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor—development credits, unused graphics analysis)
